ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the network device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of network device (e.g., router, switch, firewall) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the network device */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the network device */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model of the network device */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the network device */,
  "vendor" TEXT /* Vendor of the network device */,
  "software_version" TEXT /* Software version running on the device */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last time the device's data was updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the device (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_protocols" (
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network protocol */,
  "protocol_name" TEXT /* Name of the network protocol */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the network protocol */,
  "port_range" TEXT /* Range of ports used by the protocol */,
  "encryption" INTEGER /* Whether the protocol supports encryption */,
  "performance_impact" TEXT /* Brief description of the protocol's impact on network performance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protocol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_traffic" (
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network traffic record */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network device that generated the traffic */,
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network protocol used */,
  "sent" TEXT /* Number of packets sent */,
  "received" TEXT /* Number of packets received */,
  "returned" TEXT /* Number of packets returned */,
  "frames" INTEGER /* Number of frames involved in the communication */,
  "nodes" INTEGER /* Number of nodes involved in the communication */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Time when the traffic was generated */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the source device */,
  "destination_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the destination device */,
  "traffic_type" TEXT /* Type of traffic (e.g., data, voice, video) */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the traffic (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("traffic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_protocol_id FOREIGN KEY ("protocol_id") REFERENCES network_protocols ("protocol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_latency" (
  "latency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network latency record */,
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network traffic record that generated the latency */,
  "average_latency" REAL /* Average latency in milliseconds */,
  "packet_loss" REAL /* Packet loss as a percentage */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Time when the latency was measured */,
  "source_device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source device */,
  "destination_device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the destination device */,
  "min_latency" REAL /* Minimum latency observed */,
  "max_latency" REAL /* Maximum latency observed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("latency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_latency_destination_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("destination_device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_latency_traffic_id FOREIGN KEY ("traffic_id") REFERENCES network_traffic ("traffic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_latency_source_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network analytics record */,
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network traffic record that generated the analytics */,
  "pattern" TEXT /* Traffic pattern (e.g., periodic, aperiodic) */,
  "trend" TEXT /* Traffic trend (e.g., increasing, decreasing) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Time when the analytics were generated */,
  "source_device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source device */,
  "destination_device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the destination device */,
  "pattern_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the traffic pattern */,
  "trend_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the traffic trend */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_analytics_source_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_analytics_traffic_id FOREIGN KEY ("traffic_id") REFERENCES network_traffic ("traffic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_analytics_destination_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("destination_device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_security" (
  "security_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network security record */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network device that generated the security record */,
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network protocol used */,
  "authentication" TEXT /* Authentication method (e.g., username/password, certificate-based) */,
  "access_control" TEXT /* Access control method (e.g., role-based, attribute-based) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Time when the security record was generated */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the source device */,
  "destination_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the destination device */,
  "security_policy" TEXT /* Security policy applied */,
  "security_status" TEXT /* Current status of the security (e.g., secure, compromised) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("security_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_security_protocol_id FOREIGN KEY ("protocol_id") REFERENCES network_protocols ("protocol_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_security_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_configuration" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network configuration record */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network device that generated the configuration record */,
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network protocol used */,
  "settings" TEXT /* Device settings (e.g., IP address, subnet mask) */,
  "parameters" TEXT /* Protocol parameters (e.g., MTU, TTL) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Time when the configuration was set */,
  "configuration_status" TEXT /* Status of the configuration (e.g., applied, pending, failed) */,
  "configuration_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_configuration_protocol_id FOREIGN KEY ("protocol_id") REFERENCES network_protocols ("protocol_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_configuration_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_faults" (
  "fault_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network fault record */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network device that generated the fault record */,
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network protocol used */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message */,
  "debug_log" TEXT /* Debug log */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Time when the fault was detected */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the source device */,
  "destination_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the destination device */,
  "fault_type" TEXT /* Type of fault (e.g., hardware, software, configuration) */,
  "fault_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the fault */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fault_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_faults_protocol_id FOREIGN KEY ("protocol_id") REFERENCES network_protocols ("protocol_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_faults_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network performance record */,
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network traffic record that generated the performance */,
  "throughput" REAL /* Throughput in Mbps */,
  "jitter" REAL /* Jitter in milliseconds */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Time when the performance was measured */,
  "source_device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source device */,
  "destination_device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the destination device */,
  "min_throughput" REAL /* Minimum throughput observed */,
  "max_throughput" REAL /* Maximum throughput observed */,
  "min_jitter" REAL /* Minimum jitter observed */,
  "max_jitter" REAL /* Maximum jitter observed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_performance_destination_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("destination_device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_performance_traffic_id FOREIGN KEY ("traffic_id") REFERENCES network_traffic ("traffic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_performance_source_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time */,
  "last_logout" TEXT /* Last logout time */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network user that started the session */,
  "login_time" TEXT /* Login time */,
  "logout_time" TEXT /* Logout time */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address used for the session */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string (e.g., browser, device) */,
  "session_status" TEXT /* Status of the session (e.g., active, expired) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES network_users ("user_id")
)
